hooks.prompt: Look at the assistant's most recent message in this conversation — the one about to be shown to the user. Judge only the prose (explanatory sentences), not code blocks, file paths, command output, proper nouns, or technical loanwords inside otherwise-Japanese sentences. Respond with {"ok": false, "reason": "<one short sentence, in Japanese, telling Claude to rewrite the response in Japanese>"} only if the prose is clearly written in English or another non-Japanese language. If there is no prose to judge (e.g. the message is pure code/output), or if you are unsure, respond with {"ok": true}.

Look at the assistant's most recent message in this conversation — the one about to be shown to the user. This may be running in Claude Code's focus/background mode, where the user never saw anything Claude said or did before this final message. Respond with {"ok": false, "reason": "<one short sentence, in Japanese, telling Claude to restate the missing content inline instead of pointing back at it>"} only if the message clearly refers back to something said, decided, or found earlier (e.g. "as I mentioned above", "regarding what I just said", "先ほどの件について", "上記の通り", "前述の") without restating that content, leaving the user with no way to know what it's talking about. Ordinary references within a normal back-and-forth conversation are fine — do not flag those. If you are unsure whether this is a real problem, respond with {"ok": true}.
